articles.headline: Monday Blues Are More Than a Mood. They're in Your Biology.
articles.body: A new study from the University of Hong Kong tracked thousands of older adults and found that people who dread Mondays carry elevated cortisol in their hair for months afterward. That's not just a bad vibe — it's a measurable stress hormone hanging around long after the weekend ends. Researchers found the effect holds even for retirees, which means it's not just your job causing it. Society's calendar is literally wired into your stress physiology. And chronically elevated cortisol raises your cardiovascular risk over time.
articles.takeaway: Build a Sunday ritual that sets a positive tone for Monday. Even 10 minutes of intentional calm — a walk, a short workout, quiet coffee — can interrupt the weekly stress cycle before it starts.

articles.topic: GLP-1 medications testosterone levels men obesity
articles.headline: Weight Loss Drugs Are Also Quietly Fixing Low Testosterone
articles.body: Here's a side effect nobody's complaining about. A study presented at ENDO 2025 — the Endocrine Society's annual meeting — found that GLP-1 medications like semaglutide and tirzepatide significantly raised testosterone in men with obesity or type 2 diabetes. Among men who lost around 10% of their body weight on these drugs, the share with normal testosterone levels jumped from 53% to 77%. Researchers say low testosterone can be reversed with weight loss alone — and these medications are making that easier.
articles.takeaway: If you're a man struggling with low energy, reduced muscle mass, or low libido — and you carry excess weight — talk to your doctor about how weight loss (by any sustainable means) could be the first move before considering hormone therapy.

articles.topic: ZOE microbiome health ranking diet study 2025
articles.headline: Scientists Just Ranked 661 Gut Bacteria by How Good They Are for You
articles.body: A massive new study in Nature analyzed gut microbiome data from over 34,000 people in the US and UK — and built the first large-scale ranking of gut bacteria by their association with health and diet quality. The result? People with favorable gut bacteria had lower BMI and fewer diseases. Those with unfavorable bacteria were more likely to have obesity-related conditions. The good news: when people improved their diets in clinical trials, their beneficial bacteria increased. Your gut responds to what you feed it.
articles.takeaway: You don't need a microbiome test to start. More fiber, more whole foods, and more dietary variety are still the most reliable ways to shift your gut bacteria in the right direction.

articles.topic: DOMS recovery strategies massage cold water immersion comparison
articles.headline: Cold Plunges vs. Massage vs. Stretching: What Actually Kills Soreness Best?
articles.body: A 2025 randomized controlled trial in Frontiers in Physiology put five popular recovery methods head to head for muscle soreness relief: massage, cold water immersion, vibration therapy, static stretching, and electrical stimulation. The finding? No single method wins at every stage. Cold water works best in the first 24 hours to control inflammation. Massage takes over from 24 to 48 hours. Stretching helps most from 48 to 72 hours when stiffness peaks. Researchers recommend a phased approach rather than picking one and sticking with it.
articles.takeaway: Think of recovery as a timeline, not a single tool. Ice or cold water right after a hard session, then massage or rolling the next day, then stretching on day three. Stack them and you'll bounce back faster.

workout.title: Tuesday Tempo Ladder
workout.warmup: Start with 8 minutes at an easy, conversational pace — you should be able to say a full sentence without gasping. Finish the warmup with 4 short 10-second stride-outs at moderate effort to prime your legs.

exercises.name: Easy Base Run
exercises.notes: Stay fully conversational. This is your foundation — resist the urge to push here.

exercises.name: Tempo Interval 1
exercises.notes: Comfortably hard effort — you can speak in short phrases but not hold a full conversation. Think 7 out of 10 effort.

exercises.name: Easy Recovery Run
exercises.notes: Drop back to easy, conversational pace. Let your breathing settle completely before the next interval.

exercises.name: Tempo Interval 2
exercises.notes: Same 7/10 effort as the first tempo block. Focus on smooth turnover and relaxed shoulders — not speed.

exercises.name: Easy Cool-Down Run
exercises.notes: Fully easy. Let your heart rate come down naturally before you stop moving.

workout.cooldown: Walk for 3 to 5 minutes after finishing. Then spend 5 minutes on standing quad, calf, and hip flexor stretches — your hips and calves take the most beating on tempo runs.

product.name: HOKA Clifton 9
product.blurb: Max cushion for easy days and long runs. Keeps your legs fresh for hard sessions.

meal.title: Turkey Taco Rice Bowl
ingredients: 1 cup cooked brown rice or microwaveable rice pouch
1/2 cup black beans, rinsed and drained
Optional: sliced avocado, plain Greek yogurt instead of sour cream, fresh cilantro
meal.instructions: 1. Cook ground turkey in a skillet over medium-high heat, breaking it apart as it browns (about 6 to 8 minutes). Season with cumin, chili powder, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. 2. Add black beans, corn, and salsa to the skillet. Stir and heat through for 2 to 3 minutes. 3. Warm your rice in the microwave if using a pouch. 4. Build your bowl: rice on the bottom, turkey mixture on top, finish with cheese and any optional toppings.
meal.tip: Batch cook the turkey taco mixture on Sunday and it keeps in the fridge for 4 days. Throw it on rice, in a wrap, or over greens — one cook, four meals done.

product.name: Greater Goods Digital Food Scale
product.blurb: Measure your food for two weeks and you will understand your body better than any diet book.
